One-bay nagare-zukuri (Okutsushima Shrine) |Alternate name = Shima no Ōmiya |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|WD=Q2521|1=April 18}} |Rituals = Torch Festival ({{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ōshima Shrine
Okutsushima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine''' (おおしまじんじゃおくつしまじんじゃ / おきつしまじんじゃ, '''Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitatsuda-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It consists of '''Ōshima Shrine''' and '''Okutsushima Shrine''', both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. The former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Ōshima Shrine) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Okutsushima Shrine) === Shrine crest === Tortoiseshell floral diamond === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}. Ōshima Shrine and Okutsushima Shrine were originally enshrined at separate locations, but were relocated to the same site prior to the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Enbun|Q1145071|WD=Q1145071|1=Enbun}} ({{ill|lt=1361|Q6159|WD=Q6159|1=1361}}). Ōshima Shrine is classified as a Shikinaisha minor shrine, while Okutsushima Shrine is a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. According to the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank Upper Grade. In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}), it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine. === Auxiliary and branch shrines === * Within the precincts ** Hifure Shrine ** Hiyoshi Shrine ** Shinomiya Shrine ** Kisai Shrine ** Gyōji Shrine ** Hakusan Shrine ** Aya Shrine * Outside the precincts ** Ikejina Shrine ** Wakamikoshi Shrine ** Ame Shrine ** Wakamiya Shrine ** Dōdō Shrine === Festivals === * Torch Festival – held on April 17. * Annual Festival – held on April 18. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national designation) === * Seated wooden statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto (Sculpture) – Heian period work. Designated September 2, 1909 (Meiji 42){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|3328|Seated Wooden Statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Chrysanthemum mother-of-pearl inlaid saddle, black lacquered saddle, black lacquered stirrups (Crafts) – Late Heian to Kamakura period works. Designated June 9, 1981 (Shōwa 56){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|7223|{Chrysanthemum Mother-of-Pearl Inlaid Saddle (front ring missing) / Black Lacquered Saddle / Black Lacquered Stirrups}}}. * Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine documents (222 items): 1 scroll, 32 volumes, 3 sheets (Calligraphy) – Documents from the Kamakura to Edo periods. Designated June 6, 1987 (Shōwa 62){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9093|Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine Documents (222 items)}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=278 Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine] – Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おおしましんしやおきつしましんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:近江八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:近江八幡市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

One-bay nagare-zukuri (Okutsushima Shrine) |Alternate name = Shima no Ōmiya |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|WD=Q2521|1=April 18}} |Rituals = Torch Festival ({{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ōshima Shrine
Okutsushima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine''' (おおしまじんじゃおくつしまじんじゃ / おきつしまじんじゃ, '''Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitatsuda-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It consists of '''Ōshima Shrine''' and '''Okutsushima Shrine''', both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. The former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Ōshima Shrine) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Okutsushima Shrine) === Shrine crest === Tortoiseshell floral diamond === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}. Ōshima Shrine and Okutsushima Shrine were originally enshrined at separate locations, but were relocated to the same site prior to the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Enbun|Q1145071|WD=Q1145071|1=Enbun}} ({{ill|lt=1361|Q6159|WD=Q6159|1=1361}}). Ōshima Shrine is classified as a Shikinaisha minor shrine, while Okutsushima Shrine is a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. According to the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank Upper Grade. In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}), it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine. === Auxiliary and branch shrines === * Within the precincts ** Hifure Shrine ** Hiyoshi Shrine ** Shinomiya Shrine ** Kisai Shrine ** Gyōji Shrine ** Hakusan Shrine ** Aya Shrine * Outside the precincts ** Ikejina Shrine ** Wakamikoshi Shrine ** Ame Shrine ** Wakamiya Shrine ** Dōdō Shrine === Festivals === * Torch Festival – held on April 17. * Annual Festival – held on April 18. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national designation) === * Seated wooden statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto (Sculpture) – Heian period work. Designated September 2, 1909 (Meiji 42){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|3328|Seated Wooden Statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Chrysanthemum mother-of-pearl inlaid saddle, black lacquered saddle, black lacquered stirrups (Crafts) – Late Heian to Kamakura period works. Designated June 9, 1981 (Shōwa 56){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|7223|{Chrysanthemum Mother-of-Pearl Inlaid Saddle (front ring missing) / Black Lacquered Saddle / Black Lacquered Stirrups}}}. * Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine documents (222 items): 1 scroll, 32 volumes, 3 sheets (Calligraphy) – Documents from the Kamakura to Edo periods. Designated June 6, 1987 (Shōwa 62){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9093|Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine Documents (222 items)}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=278 Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine] – Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おおしましんしやおきつしましんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:近江八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:近江八幡市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおしまじんじゃ・おきつしまじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2160005006958 == Commons category (P373) == * Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|lt=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|WD=Q135193530|1=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県近江八幡市北津田町529', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} [[JA:大嶋神社奥津嶋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11435100}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine |Image = [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, haisho.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}} Kitatsuda-chō 529 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Ōkuninushi-no-Kami
Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto |Shrine status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha / Shōsha)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Trad.) reign of Emperor Seimu |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Ōshima Shrine)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (Okutsushima Shrine) |Alternate name = Shima no Ōmiya |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|WD=Q2521|1=April 18}} |Rituals = Torch Festival ({{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ōshima Shrine
Okutsushima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine''' (おおしまじんじゃおくつしまじんじゃ / おきつしまじんじゃ, '''Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitatsuda-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It consists of '''Ōshima Shrine''' and '''Okutsushima Shrine''', both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. The former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Ōshima Shrine) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Okutsushima Shrine) === Shrine crest === Tortoiseshell floral diamond === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}. Ōshima Shrine and Okutsushima Shrine were originally enshrined at separate locations, but were relocated to the same site prior to the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Enbun|Q1145071|WD=Q1145071|1=Enbun}} ({{ill|lt=1361|Q6159|WD=Q6159|1=1361}}). Ōshima Shrine is classified as a Shikinaisha minor shrine, while Okutsushima Shrine is a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. According to the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank Upper Grade. In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}), it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine. === Auxiliary and branch shrines === * Within the precincts ** Hifure Shrine ** Hiyoshi Shrine ** Shinomiya Shrine ** Kisai Shrine ** Gyōji Shrine ** Hakusan Shrine ** Aya Shrine * Outside the precincts ** Ikejina Shrine ** Wakamikoshi Shrine ** Ame Shrine ** Wakamiya Shrine ** Dōdō Shrine === Festivals === * Torch Festival – held on April 17. * Annual Festival – held on April 18. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national designation) === * Seated wooden statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto (Sculpture) – Heian period work. Designated September 2, 1909 (Meiji 42){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|3328|Seated Wooden Statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Chrysanthemum mother-of-pearl inlaid saddle, black lacquered saddle, black lacquered stirrups (Crafts) – Late Heian to Kamakura period works. Designated June 9, 1981 (Shōwa 56){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|7223|{Chrysanthemum Mother-of-Pearl Inlaid Saddle (front ring missing) / Black Lacquered Saddle / Black Lacquered Stirrups}}}. * Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine documents (222 items): 1 scroll, 32 volumes, 3 sheets (Calligraphy) – Documents from the Kamakura to Edo periods. Designated June 6, 1987 (Shōwa 62){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9093|Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine Documents (222 items)}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=278 Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine] – Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おおしましんしやおきつしましんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:近江八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:近江八幡市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおしまじんじゃ・おきつしまじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2160005006958 == Commons category (P373) == * Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|lt=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|WD=Q135193530|1=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県近江八幡市北津田町529', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} [[JA:大嶋神社奥津嶋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11435100}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine |Image = [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, haisho.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}} Kitatsuda-chō 529 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Ōkuninushi-no-Kami
Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto |Shrine status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha / Shōsha)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Trad.) reign of Emperor Seimu |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Ōshima Shrine)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (Okutsushima Shrine) |Alternate name = Shima no Ōmiya |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|WD=Q2521|1=April 18}} |Rituals = Torch Festival ({{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ōshima Shrine
Okutsushima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine''' (おおしまじんじゃおくつしまじんじゃ / おきつしまじんじゃ, '''Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitatsuda-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It consists of '''Ōshima Shrine''' and '''Okutsushima Shrine''', both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. The former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Ōshima Shrine) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Okutsushima Shrine) === Shrine crest === Tortoiseshell floral diamond === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}. Ōshima Shrine and Okutsushima Shrine were originally enshrined at separate locations, but were relocated to the same site prior to the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Enbun|Q1145071|WD=Q1145071|1=Enbun}} ({{ill|lt=1361|Q6159|WD=Q6159|1=1361}}). Ōshima Shrine is classified as a Shikinaisha minor shrine, while Okutsushima Shrine is a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. According to the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank Upper Grade. In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}), it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine. === Auxiliary and branch shrines === * Within the precincts ** Hifure Shrine ** Hiyoshi Shrine ** Shinomiya Shrine ** Kisai Shrine ** Gyōji Shrine ** Hakusan Shrine ** Aya Shrine * Outside the precincts ** Ikejina Shrine ** Wakamikoshi Shrine ** Ame Shrine ** Wakamiya Shrine ** Dōdō Shrine === Festivals === * Torch Festival – held on April 17. * Annual Festival – held on April 18. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national designation) === * Seated wooden statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto (Sculpture) – Heian period work. Designated September 2, 1909 (Meiji 42){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|3328|Seated Wooden Statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Chrysanthemum mother-of-pearl inlaid saddle, black lacquered saddle, black lacquered stirrups (Crafts) – Late Heian to Kamakura period works. Designated June 9, 1981 (Shōwa 56){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|7223|{Chrysanthemum Mother-of-Pearl Inlaid Saddle (front ring missing) / Black Lacquered Saddle / Black Lacquered Stirrups}}}. * Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine documents (222 items): 1 scroll, 32 volumes, 3 sheets (Calligraphy) – Documents from the Kamakura to Edo periods. Designated June 6, 1987 (Shōwa 62){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9093|Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine Documents (222 items)}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=278 Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine] – Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おおしましんしやおきつしましんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:近江八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:近江八幡市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:moved from a redlink]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおしまじんじゃ・おきつしまじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2160005006958 == Commons category (P373) == * Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|lt=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|WD=Q135193530|1=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県近江八幡市北津田町529', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} [[JA:大嶋神社奥津嶋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11435100}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine |Image = [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, haisho.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}} Kitatsuda-chō 529 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Ōkuninushi-no-Kami
Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto |Shrine status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha / Shōsha)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Trad.) reign of Emperor Seimu |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Ōshima Shrine)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (Okutsushima Shrine) |Alternate name = Shima no Ōmiya |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|WD=Q2521|1=April 18}} |Rituals = Torch Festival ({{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ōshima Shrine
Okutsushima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine''' (おおしまじんじゃおくつしまじんじゃ / おきつしまじんじゃ, '''Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitatsuda-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It consists of '''Ōshima Shrine''' and '''Okutsushima Shrine''', both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. The former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Ōshima Shrine) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Okutsushima Shrine) === Shrine crest === Tortoiseshell floral diamond === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}. Ōshima Shrine and Okutsushima Shrine were originally enshrined at separate locations, but were relocated to the same site prior to the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Enbun|Q1145071|WD=Q1145071|1=Enbun}} ({{ill|lt=1361|Q6159|WD=Q6159|1=1361}}). Ōshima Shrine is classified as a Shikinaisha minor shrine, while Okutsushima Shrine is a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. According to the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank Upper Grade. In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}), it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine. === Auxiliary and branch shrines === * Within the precincts ** Hifure Shrine ** Hiyoshi Shrine ** Shinomiya Shrine ** Kisai Shrine ** Gyōji Shrine ** Hakusan Shrine ** Aya Shrine * Outside the precincts ** Ikejina Shrine ** Wakamikoshi Shrine ** Ame Shrine ** Wakamiya Shrine ** Dōdō Shrine === Festivals === * Torch Festival – held on April 17. * Annual Festival – held on April 18. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national designation) === * Seated wooden statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto (Sculpture) – Heian period work. Designated September 2, 1909 (Meiji 42){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|3328|Seated Wooden Statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Chrysanthemum mother-of-pearl inlaid saddle, black lacquered saddle, black lacquered stirrups (Crafts) – Late Heian to Kamakura period works. Designated June 9, 1981 (Shōwa 56){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|7223|{Chrysanthemum Mother-of-Pearl Inlaid Saddle (front ring missing) / Black Lacquered Saddle / Black Lacquered Stirrups}}}. * Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine documents (222 items): 1 scroll, 32 volumes, 3 sheets (Calligraphy) – Documents from the Kamakura to Edo periods. Designated June 6, 1987 (Shōwa 62){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9093|Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine Documents (222 items)}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=278 Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine] – Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おおしましんしやおきつしましんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:近江八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:近江八幡市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおしまじんじゃ・おきつしまじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2160005006958 == Commons category (P373) == * Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|lt=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|WD=Q135193530|1=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県近江八幡市北津田町529', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} [[JA:大嶋神社奥津嶋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11435100}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine |Image = [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, haisho.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}} Kitatsuda-chō 529 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Ōkuninushi-no-Kami
Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto |Shrine status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha / Shōsha)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Trad.) reign of Emperor Seimu |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Ōshima Shrine)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (Okutsushima Shrine) |Alternate name = Shima no Ōmiya |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|WD=Q2521|1=April 18}} |Rituals = Torch Festival ({{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ōshima Shrine
Okutsushima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine''' (おおしまじんじゃおくつしまじんじゃ / おきつしまじんじゃ, '''Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitatsuda-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It consists of '''Ōshima Shrine''' and '''Okutsushima Shrine''', both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. The former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Ōshima Shrine) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Okutsushima Shrine) === Shrine crest === Tortoiseshell floral diamond === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}. Ōshima Shrine and Okutsushima Shrine were originally enshrined at separate locations, but were relocated to the same site prior to the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Enbun|Q1145071|WD=Q1145071|1=Enbun}} ({{ill|lt=1361|Q6159|WD=Q6159|1=1361}}). Ōshima Shrine is classified as a Shikinaisha minor shrine, while Okutsushima Shrine is a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. According to the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank Upper Grade. In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}), it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine. === Auxiliary and branch shrines === * Within the precincts ** Hifure Shrine ** Hiyoshi Shrine ** Shinomiya Shrine ** Kisai Shrine ** Gyōji Shrine ** Hakusan Shrine ** Aya Shrine * Outside the precincts ** Ikejina Shrine ** Wakamikoshi Shrine ** Ame Shrine ** Wakamiya Shrine ** Dōdō Shrine === Festivals === * Torch Festival – held on April 17. * Annual Festival – held on April 18. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national designation) === * Seated wooden statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto (Sculpture) – Heian period work. Designated September 2, 1909 (Meiji 42){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|3328|Seated Wooden Statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Chrysanthemum mother-of-pearl inlaid saddle, black lacquered saddle, black lacquered stirrups (Crafts) – Late Heian to Kamakura period works. Designated June 9, 1981 (Shōwa 56){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|7223|{Chrysanthemum Mother-of-Pearl Inlaid Saddle (front ring missing) / Black Lacquered Saddle / Black Lacquered Stirrups}}}. * Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine documents (222 items): 1 scroll, 32 volumes, 3 sheets (Calligraphy) – Documents from the Kamakura to Edo periods. Designated June 6, 1987 (Shōwa 62){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9093|Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine Documents (222 items)}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=278 Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine] – Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おおしましんしやおきつしましんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:近江八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:近江八幡市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおしまじんじゃ・おきつしまじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2160005006958 == Commons category (P373) == * Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|lt=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|WD=Q135193530|1=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県近江八幡市北津田町529', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} [[JA:大嶋神社奥津嶋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11435100}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine |Image = [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, haisho.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}} Kitatsuda-chō 529 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Ōkuninushi-no-Kami
Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto |Shrine status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha / Shōsha)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Trad.) reign of Emperor Seimu |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Ōshima Shrine)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (Okutsushima Shrine) |Alternate name = Shima no Ōmiya |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|WD=Q2521|1=April 18}} |Rituals = Torch Festival ({{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ōshima Shrine
Okutsushima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine''' (おおしまじんじゃおくつしまじんじゃ / おきつしまじんじゃ, '''Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitatsuda-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It consists of '''Ōshima Shrine''' and '''Okutsushima Shrine''', both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. The former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Ōshima Shrine) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Okutsushima Shrine) === Shrine crest === Tortoiseshell floral diamond === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}. Ōshima Shrine and Okutsushima Shrine were originally enshrined at separate locations, but were relocated to the same site prior to the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Enbun|Q1145071|WD=Q1145071|1=Enbun}} ({{ill|lt=1361|Q6159|WD=Q6159|1=1361}}). Ōshima Shrine is classified as a Shikinaisha minor shrine, while Okutsushima Shrine is a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. According to the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank Upper Grade. In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}), it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine. === Auxiliary and branch shrines === * Within the precincts ** Hifure Shrine ** Hiyoshi Shrine ** Shinomiya Shrine ** Kisai Shrine ** Gyōji Shrine ** Hakusan Shrine ** Aya Shrine * Outside the precincts ** Ikejina Shrine ** Wakamikoshi Shrine ** Ame Shrine ** Wakamiya Shrine ** Dōdō Shrine === Festivals === * Torch Festival – held on April 17. * Annual Festival – held on April 18. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national designation) === * Seated wooden statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto (Sculpture) – Heian period work. Designated September 2, 1909 (Meiji 42){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|3328|Seated Wooden Statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Chrysanthemum mother-of-pearl inlaid saddle, black lacquered saddle, black lacquered stirrups (Crafts) – Late Heian to Kamakura period works. Designated June 9, 1981 (Shōwa 56){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|7223|{Chrysanthemum Mother-of-Pearl Inlaid Saddle (front ring missing) / Black Lacquered Saddle / Black Lacquered Stirrups}}}. * Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine documents (222 items): 1 scroll, 32 volumes, 3 sheets (Calligraphy) – Documents from the Kamakura to Edo periods. Designated June 6, 1987 (Shōwa 62){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9093|Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine Documents (222 items)}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=278 Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine] – Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おおしましんしやおきつしましんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:近江八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:近江八幡市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * おおしまじんじゃ・おきつしまじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2160005006958 == Commons category (P373) == * Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|lt=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines|WD=Q135193530|1=Oshima & Okitsushima Shrines}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県近江八幡市北津田町529', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} [[JA:大嶋神社奥津嶋神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11435100}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine |Image = [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, haisho.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}} Kitatsuda-chō 529 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Ōkuninushi-no-Kami
Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto |Shrine status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Myōjin Taisha / Shōsha)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Trad.) reign of Emperor Seimu |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=three-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Ōshima Shrine)
One-bay nagare-zukuri (Okutsushima Shrine) |Alternate name = Shima no Ōmiya |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|WD=Q2521|1=April 18}} |Rituals = Torch Festival ({{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ōshima Shrine
Okutsushima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine''' (おおしまじんじゃおくつしまじんじゃ / おきつしまじんじゃ, '''Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitatsuda-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It consists of '''Ōshima Shrine''' and '''Okutsushima Shrine''', both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. The former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Ōshima Shrine) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Okutsushima Shrine) === Shrine crest === Tortoiseshell floral diamond === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}. Ōshima Shrine and Okutsushima Shrine were originally enshrined at separate locations, but were relocated to the same site prior to the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Enbun|Q1145071|WD=Q1145071|1=Enbun}} ({{ill|lt=1361|Q6159|WD=Q6159|1=1361}}). Ōshima Shrine is classified as a Shikinaisha minor shrine, while Okutsushima Shrine is a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. According to the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank Upper Grade. In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}), it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine. === Auxiliary and branch shrines === * Within the precincts ** Hifure Shrine ** Hiyoshi Shrine ** Shinomiya Shrine ** Kisai Shrine ** Gyōji Shrine ** Hakusan Shrine ** Aya Shrine * Outside the precincts ** Ikejina Shrine ** Wakamikoshi Shrine ** Ame Shrine ** Wakamiya Shrine ** Dōdō Shrine === Festivals === * Torch Festival – held on April 17. * Annual Festival – held on April 18. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national designation) === * Seated wooden statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto (Sculpture) – Heian period work. Designated September 2, 1909 (Meiji 42){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|3328|Seated Wooden Statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Chrysanthemum mother-of-pearl inlaid saddle, black lacquered saddle, black lacquered stirrups (Crafts) – Late Heian to Kamakura period works. Designated June 9, 1981 (Shōwa 56){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|7223|{Chrysanthemum Mother-of-Pearl Inlaid Saddle (front ring missing) / Black Lacquered Saddle / Black Lacquered Stirrups}}}. * Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine documents (222 items): 1 scroll, 32 volumes, 3 sheets (Calligraphy) – Documents from the Kamakura to Edo periods. Designated June 6, 1987 (Shōwa 62){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9093|Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine Documents (222 items)}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=278 Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine] – Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おおしましんしやおきつしましんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:近江八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:近江八幡市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

One-bay nagare-zukuri (Okutsushima Shrine) |Alternate name = Shima no Ōmiya |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 18|Q2521|WD=Q2521|1=April 18}} |Rituals = Torch Festival ({{ill|lt=April 17|Q2520|WD=Q2520|1=April 17}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ōshima Shrine
Okutsushima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Ōshima Shrine / Okutsushima Shrine''' (おおしまじんじゃおくつしまじんじゃ / おきつしまじんじゃ, '''Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kitatsuda-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It consists of '''Ōshima Shrine''' and '''Okutsushima Shrine''', both of which are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. The former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Ōshima Shrine) *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Okutsushimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Okutsushima Shrine) === Shrine crest === Tortoiseshell floral diamond === History === According to shrine tradition, it was enshrined by {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}. Ōshima Shrine and Okutsushima Shrine were originally enshrined at separate locations, but were relocated to the same site prior to the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Enbun|Q1145071|WD=Q1145071|1=Enbun}} ({{ill|lt=1361|Q6159|WD=Q6159|1=1361}}). Ōshima Shrine is classified as a Shikinaisha minor shrine, while Okutsushima Shrine is a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. According to the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}', in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank Upper Grade. In the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}}), it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine. === Auxiliary and branch shrines === * Within the precincts ** Hifure Shrine ** Hiyoshi Shrine ** Shinomiya Shrine ** Kisai Shrine ** Gyōji Shrine ** Hakusan Shrine ** Aya Shrine * Outside the precincts ** Ikejina Shrine ** Wakamikoshi Shrine ** Ame Shrine ** Wakamiya Shrine ** Dōdō Shrine === Festivals === * Torch Festival – held on April 17. * Annual Festival – held on April 18. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (national designation) === * Seated wooden statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto (Sculpture) – Heian period work. Designated September 2, 1909 (Meiji 42){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|3328|Seated Wooden Statue of Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}}. * Chrysanthemum mother-of-pearl inlaid saddle, black lacquered saddle, black lacquered stirrups (Crafts) – Late Heian to Kamakura period works. Designated June 9, 1981 (Shōwa 56){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|7223|{Chrysanthemum Mother-of-Pearl Inlaid Saddle (front ring missing) / Black Lacquered Saddle / Black Lacquered Stirrups}}}. * Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine documents (222 items): 1 scroll, 32 volumes, 3 sheets (Calligraphy) – Documents from the Kamakura to Edo periods. Designated June 6, 1987 (Shōwa 62){{National Cultural Properties Database|201|9093|Ōshima and Okutsushima Shrine Documents (222 items)}}. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Oshima-Okutsushima-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=278 Ōshima–Okutsushima Shrine] – Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōshima & Okitsushima Shrine]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おおしましんしやおきつしましんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:近江八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:近江八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:近江八幡市の重要文化財]]
